questions.question: Solve for all values of x: |2x - 5| = |x + 3|
questions.explanation: When two absolute values are equal, either the expressions inside are equal or they're opposites. Case 1: 2x - 5 = x + 3 gives x = 8. Case 2: 2x - 5 = -(x + 3) gives 3x = 2, so x = 2/3. Check both in the original.

questions.question: Solve the inequality: |3x - 7| > 8
questions.explanation: Absolute value inequality |A| > B means A > B or A < -B. So 3x - 7 > 8 gives x > 5, and 3x - 7 < -8 gives x < -1/3. Write as union of two rays.

questions.question: For what value of c does |4x - c| = 6 have exactly one solution?
questions.answer: There is no such value of c
questions.explanation: |4x - c| = 6 always has two solutions (x = (c+6)/4 and x = (c-6)/4) unless we restrict the domain. An absolute value equation equals a positive constant always has two solutions or none—never exactly one unless the constant is 0.

questions.question: The distance from a number x to 2 is at most 5. Write an absolute value inequality and solve it.
questions.explanation: Distance from x to 2 is |x - 2|. The inequality |x - 2| ≤ 5 expands to -5 ≤ x - 2 ≤ 5, so -3 ≤ x ≤ 7 (a closed interval).

questions.question: Solve: |x + 2| + |x - 4| = 8
questions.explanation: Split into regions: x < -2, -2 ≤ x < 4, x ≥ 4. For x < -2: -(x+2) - (x-4) = 8 gives -2x + 2 = 8, so x = -3. For x ≥ 4: (x+2) + (x-4) = 8 gives 2x - 2 = 8, so x = 5. The region [-2, 4) gives 6 = 8 (no solution).

questions.question: If |2x - 3| ≤ 7 and 3x + 1 > 10, find the range of x that satisfies both conditions.
questions.explanation: From |2x - 3| ≤ 7: -7 ≤ 2x - 3 ≤ 7 gives -2 ≤ x ≤ 5. From 3x + 1 > 10: x > 3. The intersection is 3 < x ≤ 5.
